concepts.label: Atom
concepts.definition: The smallest unit of matter that retains the properties of an element

concepts.label: Element
concepts.definition: A pure substance consisting of only one type of atom

concepts.label: Periodic Table
concepts.definition: A table organizing all known elements by atomic number and chemical properties

concepts.label: Atomic Number
concepts.definition: The number of protons in an atom's nucleus

concepts.label: Atomic Mass
concepts.definition: The average mass of atoms of an element, measured in atomic mass units

concepts.label: Proton
concepts.definition: A positively charged subatomic particle found in the nucleus

concepts.label: Neutron
concepts.definition: A neutral subatomic particle found in the nucleus

concepts.label: Electron
concepts.definition: A negatively charged subatomic particle orbiting the nucleus

concepts.label: Nucleus
concepts.definition: The dense central core of an atom containing protons and neutrons

concepts.label: Electron Shell
concepts.definition: The regions around the nucleus where electrons are likely to be found

concepts.label: Valence Electrons
concepts.definition: The electrons in the outermost shell of an atom

concepts.label: Ion
concepts.definition: An atom or molecule that has gained or lost electrons, giving it a net charge

concepts.label: Cation
concepts.definition: A positively charged ion formed by losing electrons

concepts.label: Anion
concepts.definition: A negatively charged ion formed by gaining electrons

concepts.label: Isotope
concepts.definition: Atoms of the same element with different numbers of neutrons

concepts.label: Chemical Bond
concepts.definition: The attractive force that holds atoms together in molecules or compounds

concepts.label: Ionic Bond
concepts.definition: A chemical bond formed by the transfer of electrons between atoms

concepts.label: Covalent Bond
concepts.definition: A chemical bond formed by sharing electrons between atoms

concepts.label: Metallic Bond
concepts.definition: A chemical bond where electrons move freely among metal atoms

concepts.label: Hydrogen Bond
concepts.definition: A weak intermolecular force between hydrogen and electronegative atoms

concepts.label: Van der Waals Forces
concepts.definition: Weak intermolecular forces including dipole-dipole and London dispersion forces

concepts.label: Molecule
concepts.definition: A group of atoms bonded together, representing the smallest unit of a compound

concepts.label: Compound
concepts.definition: A substance composed of two or more different elements chemically bonded

concepts.label: Mixture
concepts.definition: A combination of two or more substances that are not chemically bonded

concepts.label: Solution
concepts.definition: A homogeneous mixture where one substance is dissolved in another

concepts.label: Solvent
concepts.definition: The substance that dissolves the solute in a solution

concepts.label: Solute
concepts.definition: The substance that is dissolved in a solvent

concepts.label: Concentration
concepts.definition: The amount of solute present in a given amount of solution

concepts.label: Molarity
concepts.definition: The number of moles of solute per liter of solution

concepts.label: Mole
concepts.definition: A unit of measurement equal to 6.022 × 10²³ particles

concepts.label: Avogadro's Number
concepts.definition: The number of particles in one mole: 6.022 × 10²³

concepts.label: Molar Mass
concepts.definition: The mass of one mole of a substance, expressed in grams per mole

concepts.label: Chemical Equation
concepts.definition: A symbolic representation of a chemical reaction showing reactants and products

concepts.label: Reactant
concepts.definition: A substance that undergoes change in a chemical reaction

concepts.label: Product
concepts.definition: A substance formed as a result of a chemical reaction

concepts.label: Chemical Reaction
concepts.definition: A process where substances are transformed into different substances

concepts.label: Synthesis Reaction
concepts.definition: A reaction where two or more simple substances combine to form a complex substance

concepts.label: Decomposition Reaction
concepts.definition: A reaction where a complex substance breaks down into simpler substances

concepts.label: Single Displacement
concepts.definition: A reaction where one element replaces another in a compound

concepts.label: Double Displacement
concepts.definition: A reaction where two compounds exchange ions to form new compounds

concepts.label: Combustion Reaction
concepts.definition: A reaction where a substance combines with oxygen, often producing heat and light

concepts.label: Oxidation
concepts.definition: A process where a substance loses electrons or gains oxygen

concepts.label: Reduction
concepts.definition: A process where a substance gains electrons or loses oxygen

concepts.label: Redox Reaction
concepts.definition: A reaction involving both oxidation and reduction processes

concepts.label: Catalyst
concepts.definition: A substance that increases the rate of reaction without being consumed

concepts.label: Activation Energy
concepts.definition: The minimum energy required for a chemical reaction to occur

concepts.label: Reaction Rate
concepts.definition: The speed at which reactants are converted to products in a chemical reaction

concepts.label: Chemical Equilibrium
concepts.definition: The state where forward and reverse reaction rates are equal

concepts.label: Le Chatelier's Principle
concepts.definition: When a system at equilibrium is disturbed, it shifts to counteract the disturbance

concepts.label: Acid
concepts.definition: A substance that donates protons (H⁺) or accepts electrons

concepts.label: Base
concepts.definition: A substance that accepts protons (H⁺) or donates electrons

concepts.label: pH
concepts.definition: A scale measuring the acidity or basicity of a solution from 0 to 14

concepts.label: Neutralization
concepts.definition: A reaction between an acid and base to form water and a salt

concepts.label: Salt
concepts.definition: An ionic compound formed from the neutralization of an acid and base

concepts.label: Buffer
concepts.definition: A solution that resists changes in pH when small amounts of acid or base are added

concepts.label: Electronegativity
concepts.definition: The tendency of an atom to attract electrons in a chemical bond

concepts.label: Polarity
concepts.definition: The unequal distribution of electrons in a molecule, creating partial charges

concepts.label: Dipole
concepts.definition: A molecule with a positive end and a negative end due to unequal charge distribution

concepts.label: Electrochemistry
concepts.definition: The study of chemical reactions that produce or consume electricity

concepts.label: Galvanic Cell
concepts.definition: An electrochemical cell that generates electricity from spontaneous redox reactions

concepts.label: Electrolytic Cell
concepts.definition: An electrochemical cell that uses electricity to drive non-spontaneous reactions

concepts.label: Anode
concepts.definition: The electrode where oxidation occurs in an electrochemical cell

concepts.label: Cathode
concepts.definition: The electrode where reduction occurs in an electrochemical cell

concepts.label: Thermodynamics
concepts.definition: The study of energy changes in chemical reactions and processes

concepts.label: Enthalpy
concepts.definition: The total heat content of a system at constant pressure

concepts.label: Entropy
concepts.definition: A measure of the disorder or randomness in a system

concepts.label: Gibbs Free Energy
concepts.definition: The energy available to do work in a system at constant temperature and pressure

concepts.label: Endothermic Reaction
concepts.definition: A reaction that absorbs heat from the surroundings

concepts.label: Exothermic Reaction
concepts.definition: A reaction that releases heat to the surroundings

concepts.label: Phase
concepts.definition: A distinct and homogeneous form of matter separated by phase boundaries

concepts.label: Phase Transition
concepts.definition: The change of a substance from one phase to another

concepts.label: Melting Point
concepts.definition: The temperature at which a solid becomes a liquid at standard pressure

concepts.label: Boiling Point
concepts.definition: The temperature at which a liquid becomes a gas at standard pressure

concepts.label: Sublimation
concepts.definition: The direct transition from solid to gas without passing through liquid phase

concepts.label: Vapor Pressure
concepts.definition: The pressure exerted by vapor molecules above a liquid in equilibrium

concepts.label: Organic Chemistry
concepts.definition: The study of carbon-containing compounds and their properties

concepts.label: Carbon
concepts.definition: A nonmetallic element that forms the backbone of organic molecules

concepts.label: Hydrocarbon
concepts.definition: An organic compound containing only carbon and hydrogen atoms

concepts.label: Alkane
concepts.definition: A saturated hydrocarbon with single bonds between carbon atoms

concepts.label: Alkene
concepts.definition: An unsaturated hydrocarbon with at least one double bond between carbon atoms

concepts.label: Alkyne
concepts.definition: An unsaturated hydrocarbon with at least one triple bond between carbon atoms

concepts.label: Aromatic Compound
concepts.definition: A cyclic organic compound with delocalized electrons, often containing benzene rings

concepts.label: Benzene
concepts.definition: A six-membered aromatic ring with alternating double bonds

concepts.label: Functional Group
concepts.definition: A specific group of atoms that determines the chemical properties of organic molecules

concepts.label: Alcohol
concepts.definition: An organic compound containing a hydroxyl (-OH) functional group

concepts.label: Aldehyde
concepts.definition: An organic compound with a carbonyl group (-CHO) at the end of a carbon chain

concepts.label: Ketone
concepts.definition: An organic compound with a carbonyl group (C=O) within a carbon chain

concepts.label: Carboxylic Acid
concepts.definition: An organic compound containing a carboxyl group (-COOH)

concepts.label: Ester
concepts.definition: An organic compound formed by the reaction of a carboxylic acid and an alcohol

concepts.label: Amine
concepts.definition: An organic compound derived from ammonia by replacement with alkyl or aryl groups

concepts.label: Amide
concepts.definition: An organic compound containing a carbonyl group linked to a nitrogen atom

concepts.label: Isomer
concepts.definition: Compounds with the same molecular formula but different structural arrangements

concepts.label: Structural Isomer
concepts.definition: Isomers with different connectivity between atoms

concepts.label: Stereoisomer
concepts.definition: Isomers with the same connectivity but different spatial arrangements

concepts.label: Enantiomer
concepts.definition: Stereoisomers that are non-superimposable mirror images of each other

concepts.label: Chirality
concepts.definition: The property of a molecule that is not superimposable on its mirror image

concepts.label: Polymer
concepts.definition: A large molecule made up of repeating structural units called monomers

concepts.label: Monomer
concepts.definition: A small molecule that can be bonded to other identical molecules to form a polymer

concepts.label: Polymerization
concepts.definition: The process of combining monomers to form polymers

concepts.label: Addition Polymerization
concepts.definition: Polymerization where monomers add to growing chains without losing atoms

concepts.label: Condensation Polymerization
concepts.definition: Polymerization where monomers combine with loss of small molecules

concepts.label: Protein
concepts.definition: Large biological polymers made of amino acid monomers

concepts.label: Amino Acid
concepts.definition: Organic molecules containing both amino and carboxyl functional groups

concepts.label: Peptide Bond
concepts.definition: A covalent bond linking amino acids in protein chains

concepts.label: DNA
concepts.definition: Deoxyribonucleic acid, the molecule that stores genetic information

concepts.label: RNA
concepts.definition: Ribonucleic acid, involved in protein synthesis and gene expression

concepts.label: Nucleotide
concepts.definition: The building blocks of DNA and RNA, consisting of base, sugar, and phosphate

concepts.label: Carbohydrate
concepts.definition: Organic compounds consisting of carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen, often in a 1:2:1 ratio

concepts.label: Glucose
concepts.definition: A simple sugar (monosaccharide) that is the primary energy source for cells

concepts.label: Lipid
concepts.definition: Biological molecules that are largely nonpolar and include fats, oils, and waxes

concepts.label: Fatty Acid
concepts.definition: Long hydrocarbon chains with a carboxyl group, components of many lipids

concepts.label: Enzyme
concepts.definition: Biological catalysts that speed up biochemical reactions

concepts.label: Active Site
concepts.definition: The specific region of an enzyme where substrate molecules bind and react

concepts.label: Substrate
concepts.definition: The reactant molecule that binds to an enzyme's active site

concepts.label: Metabolism
concepts.definition: The sum of all chemical reactions occurring in a living organism

concepts.label: Anabolism
concepts.definition: The set of metabolic pathways that build complex molecules from simpler ones

concepts.label: Catabolism
concepts.definition: The set of metabolic pathways that break down complex molecules into simpler ones

concepts.label: ATP
concepts.definition: Adenosine triphosphate, the primary energy currency of cells

concepts.label: Photosynthesis
concepts.definition: The process by which plants convert light energy into chemical energy

concepts.label: Cellular Respiration
concepts.definition: The process by which cells break down glucose to produce ATP

concepts.label: Oxidation State
concepts.definition: The hypothetical charge an atom would have if all bonding electrons were assigned to the more electronegative atom

concepts.label: Formal Charge
concepts.definition: The charge assigned to an atom in a molecule assuming equal sharing of bonding electrons

concepts.label: Lewis Structure
concepts.definition: A diagram showing the bonding between atoms and lone electron pairs in a molecule

concepts.label: VSEPR Theory
concepts.definition: A model predicting molecular geometry based on electron pair repulsion

concepts.label: Hybridization
concepts.definition: The concept of mixing atomic orbitals to form new hybrid orbitals for bonding

concepts.label: Molecular Orbital Theory
concepts.definition: A theory describing chemical bonding using quantum mechanics and molecular orbitals

concepts.label: Crystal Lattice
concepts.definition: The regular, repeating arrangement of atoms, ions, or molecules in a crystalline solid

concepts.label: Unit Cell
concepts.definition: The smallest repeating unit in a crystal lattice

concepts.label: Ionic Crystal
concepts.definition: A crystal structure held together by ionic bonds between cations and anions

concepts.label: Covalent Crystal
concepts.definition: A crystal structure where atoms are connected by covalent bonds throughout the structure

concepts.label: Metallic Crystal
concepts.definition: A crystal structure of metal atoms held together by metallic bonding

concepts.label: Molecular Crystal
concepts.definition: A crystal structure held together by intermolecular forces between molecules

concepts.label: Allotropy
concepts.definition: The existence of an element in two or more different forms in the same physical state

concepts.label: Diamond
concepts.definition: A form of carbon with each carbon atom bonded to four others in a tetrahedral structure

concepts.label: Graphite
concepts.definition: A form of carbon with layered structure and delocalized electrons

concepts.label: Fullerene
concepts.definition: Hollow carbon molecules with cage-like structures, including buckyballs

concepts.label: Graphene
concepts.definition: A single layer of carbon atoms arranged in a hexagonal lattice

concepts.label: Nanotechnology
concepts.definition: The manipulation of matter at the molecular and atomic scale

concepts.label: Quantum Dots
concepts.definition: Semiconductor nanoparticles with size-dependent optical properties

concepts.label: Surface Chemistry
concepts.definition: The study of chemical processes that occur at surfaces and interfaces

concepts.label: Adsorption
concepts.definition: The adhesion of atoms, ions, or molecules from a gas, liquid, or dissolved solid to a surface

concepts.label: Desorption
concepts.definition: The process by which adsorbed molecules are released from a surface

concepts.label: Catalytic Converter
concepts.definition: A device that uses catalysts to reduce harmful emissions from vehicle exhaust

concepts.label: Green Chemistry
concepts.definition: The design of chemical products and processes to reduce environmental impact

concepts.label: Renewable Energy
concepts.definition: Energy from sources that are naturally replenished, such as solar and wind

concepts.label: Fuel Cell
concepts.definition: A device that converts chemical energy into electrical energy through electrochemical reactions

concepts.label: Battery
concepts.definition: A device that stores chemical energy and converts it to electrical energy

concepts.label: Semiconductor
concepts.definition: A material with electrical conductivity between that of conductor and insulator

concepts.label: Superconductor
concepts.definition: A material that can conduct electricity with zero resistance below a critical temperature

concepts.label: Liquid Crystal
concepts.definition: A state of matter with properties between conventional liquids and crystals

concepts.label: Smart Material
concepts.definition: Materials that can change their properties in response to external stimuli

concepts.label: Biomaterial
concepts.definition: Materials designed to interact with biological systems for medical purposes

concepts.label: Composite Material
concepts.definition: Materials made from two or more constituent materials with different properties

concepts.label: Pharmaceutical Chemistry
concepts.definition: The study of drugs and their interaction with biological systems

concepts.label: Drug Discovery
concepts.definition: The process of identifying and developing new therapeutic compounds

concepts.label: Pharmacokinetics
concepts.definition: The study of how the body processes drugs through absorption, distribution, metabolism, and excretion

concepts.label: Pharmacodynamics
concepts.definition: The study of how drugs affect the body and their mechanism of action

concepts.label: Chemical Kinetics
concepts.definition: The study of reaction rates and the factors that affect them

concepts.label: Rate Constant
concepts.definition: A proportionality constant in the rate equation relating reaction rate to reactant concentrations

concepts.label: Half-Life
concepts.definition: The time required for half of a reactant to be consumed in a chemical reaction

concepts.label: Arrhenius Equation
concepts.definition: An equation relating the rate constant to temperature and activation energy

concepts.label: Spectroscopy
concepts.definition: The study of the interaction between matter and electromagnetic radiation

concepts.label: Mass Spectrometry
concepts.definition: An analytical technique that measures the mass-to-charge ratio of ions

concepts.label: NMR Spectroscopy
concepts.definition: A technique that exploits magnetic properties of atomic nuclei to determine molecular structure

concepts.label: IR Spectroscopy
concepts.definition: A technique that measures absorption of infrared radiation to identify functional groups

concepts.label: UV-Vis Spectroscopy
concepts.definition: A technique measuring absorption of ultraviolet and visible light by molecules

concepts.label: Chromatography
concepts.definition: A technique for separating mixtures based on differential movement through a stationary phase

concepts.label: Gas Chromatography
concepts.definition: A chromatography technique using a gas mobile phase to separate volatile compounds

concepts.label: Liquid Chromatography
concepts.definition: A chromatography technique using a liquid mobile phase to separate compounds

concepts.label: X-ray Crystallography
concepts.definition: A technique using X-ray diffraction to determine atomic structure of crystals

concepts.label: Electrochemical Analysis
concepts.definition: Analytical methods based on electrical properties of solutions

concepts.label: Titration
concepts.definition: A quantitative analytical technique to determine concentration by neutralization

concepts.label: Indicator
concepts.definition: A substance that changes color to signal the endpoint of a titration

concepts.label: Standard Solution
concepts.definition: A solution of accurately known concentration used in analytical chemistry

concepts.label: Gravimetric Analysis
concepts.definition: A quantitative method based on measuring the mass of a pure compound

concepts.label: Stoichiometry
concepts.definition: The calculation of quantities in chemical reactions based on balanced equations

concepts.label: Limiting Reagent
concepts.definition: The reactant that is completely consumed first, limiting the amount of product formed

concepts.label: Theoretical Yield
concepts.definition: The maximum amount of product that can be formed from given amounts of reactants

concepts.label: Actual Yield
concepts.definition: The amount of product actually obtained from a chemical reaction

concepts.label: Percent Yield
concepts.definition: The ratio of actual yield to theoretical yield, expressed as a percentage

concepts.label: Chemical Formula
concepts.definition: A representation of a compound showing the types and numbers of atoms present

concepts.label: Empirical Formula
concepts.definition: The simplest whole-number ratio of atoms in a compound

concepts.label: Molecular Formula
concepts.definition: The actual number of atoms of each element in a molecule

concepts.label: Structural Formula
concepts.definition: A representation showing how atoms are connected in a molecule
